_description: Residual latency deltas between FC RTL (NL-DPE and Azure-Lily) and sim's analytical model, annotated with their root cause. Entries here are accepted as modelling-granularity deltas (FSM handshake cycles or probe-convention artifacts the analytical model does not track). A stage with a non-empty root_cause is allowed to fail the exact-equality check without breaking Phase 2.

_schema.stage: one of: feed, compute, output, reduction, activation, reduction_plus_activation, compute_aggregate, compute_first_out
_schema.delta_cycles: RTL - sim (positive means RTL slower than sim)
_schema.applies_to: list of setups and workloads this delta is known to apply to, or 'all'
_schema.arch: nl_dpe | azurelily — which architecture the entry applies to

deltas.root_cause: conv_controller FSM handshake: reg_full -> dpe_exec_signal -> nl_dpe_control=2'b11 -> DPE fires in S_WAIT_EXEC. Three pipeline flops (nl_dpe/gemv_1_channel.v lines 488-510) + 1 cycle S_WAIT_EXEC check in dpe_stub.v lines 171-198. The analytical sim (imc_core._core_bit_pipeline_row_latency) counts only the bit-serial analog pipeline, not the control handshake. The 4 cycles are purely FSM overhead, independent of bus width, crossbar size, or conversion type.

controller_scalable valid_n handshake: dpe_done -> (register) -> valid_n in controller_scalable (nl_dpe/gemv_1_channel.v controller_scalable always-block driving valid_n). The reduction adder tree and activation LUT inside fc_layer are physically streaming-pipelined with the DPE output serialize stage (26/64 cycles) so their analytical depth (log2(V)+1) is fully hidden (output >> log2(V)+1). The residual +1 cycle measured at T_validn - T_done_first is the controller_scalable FSM's dpe_done -> valid_n latching, not the reduction/activation path. Classifies as FSM modelling granularity identical in spirit to the +4 compute handshake entry above.

Force-state probe convention in tb_azurelily_fc.v: T_compute_start is captured at cycle 0 (the simulation moment when state was forced to S_COMPUTE via blocking assign in the initial block) but the FSM's first compute cycle is cycle 1 (one posedge later, when state=S_COMPUTE is sampled). Aggregate compute cost RTL = sim + 1 (one-time setup) for every (K, N). Per-output steady-state cycles RTL == sim exactly (PACKED_K + 3). Pure measurement convention; no FSM overhead introduced by the AL FC architecture itself.

Same probe-convention root cause as compute_aggregate (force-state at cycle 0, real start at cycle 1). T_first_out - T_compute_start + 1 = sim PACKED_K+3 + 1 = PACKED_K + 4. Per-output steady-state (between successive out_valid pulses) is exactly PACKED_K + 3, no residual.
